sections.title: 1. Scope
paragraphs: These terms apply to the use of Finora (the "Service"). By registering and using the Service you agree to these terms.

sections.title: 2. Registration and account
paragraphs: To use the Service you must create an account. You agree to:
list: Provide accurate and complete information
Keep your credentials confidential
Notify us promptly about unauthorized use
Create only one account per person
The Service is intended for persons aged 16 or older. Use by minors under 16 is only permitted with the express consent of a legal guardian.

sections.title: 3. Use of the Service
paragraphs: The Service helps you manage income and expenses. You may only use it for lawful purposes.
In particular, it is forbidden to:
list: Use the Service for illegal activities
Upload malware or malicious code
Attempt to gain unauthorized access
Disrupt or interfere with the Service

sections.title: 4. Privacy
paragraphs: We take data protection seriously. We process personal data according to applicable laws. For details, see our Privacy Policy.

sections.title: 5. Intellectual property
paragraphs: All rights in the Service, including design, code, and content, belong to the operator. Use of the Service does not grant rights to trademarks, logos, or protected elements.

sections.title: 6. Disclaimer
paragraphs: The Service is provided "as is". We do not guarantee:
list: Uninterrupted availability
Error-free operation
Accuracy of user-provided data
additionalParagraphs: This does not affect our liability for intent and gross negligence, nor for damages to life, body, or health. Statutory consumer rights remain unaffected.

sections.title: 7. Termination
paragraphs: You may delete your account at any time in settings. We may suspend or delete accounts that violate these terms.

sections.title: 8. Changes to the terms
paragraphs: We may update these terms from time to time. We will notify you of material changes at least 30 days before they take effect by email. Changes that substantially affect your rights require your renewed consent.

sections.title: 9. Final provisions
paragraphs: The laws of the Federal Republic of Germany apply. If any provision is invalid, the remaining provisions remain effective.

sections.title: 10. Service description
paragraphs: Finora is a free application for managing personal finances. Current features include: transaction management, budget planning, data export (CSV/PDF), newsletter and contact form.
We reserve the right to extend, restrict or introduce paid features at any time. Registered users will be notified of material changes in advance.

sections.title: 11. Prices and costs
paragraphs: The use of Finora is currently free of charge. No fees or subscription costs apply.
Should paid features be introduced in the future, they will be clearly labelled and require separate consent.

sections.title: 12. Account suspension for misuse
paragraphs: We reserve the right to temporarily suspend or permanently delete accounts if:
list: These terms of service are violated
The platform is used for unlawful purposes
Repeated or serious violations of usage rules occur
Automated access or manipulation attempts are detected
additionalParagraphs: Before permanent deletion, you will receive a notification stating the reasons and a reasonable period to respond, provided circumstances allow.

sections.title: 13. Jurisdiction
paragraphs: German law applies to disputes arising from or in connection with these terms. If the user is a consumer, mandatory consumer protection provisions of the country of residence apply.

sections.title: § 1 Data Controller (Art. 13(1)(a) GDPR)
paragraphs: The data controller responsible for data processing in this application is:
Youssef Dawod
Bahnhofstraße 1, 29614 Soltau, Germany
Email: finora@yellowdeveloper.de | Alternatively: Contact form at /contact
The controller determines the purposes and means of processing personal data.

sections.title: § 2 Data Protection Officer (Art. 13(1)(b) GDPR)
paragraphs: There is currently no legal obligation to appoint a data protection officer. For questions regarding data protection, you can reach us using the contact details above.

sections.title: § 3 Data Collected
paragraphs: In the course of using our application, we collect and process the following personal data:
list: Username (required) and email address (optional) during registration
Password (stored as a cryptographic hash, bcrypt with 12 rounds)
Financial transaction data (amount, category, description, date)
Language settings, theme preferences, and sidebar state
Privacy notice status (whether the notice has been acknowledged)

IP address, user agent, and geographic origin (country – derived offline from IP address, no third party; city is intentionally not collected as the accuracy of the database used is insufficient) on admin actions (security audit log)
Contact form data (name, email, category, message)
Newsletter subscription (email, double opt-in status)
Feedback data (star rating, optional free text, username as display name) when voluntarily submitting feedback

sections.title: § 4 Legal Bases for Processing (Art. 13(1)(c) GDPR)
paragraphs: We process personal data based on the following legal grounds:
list: Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR (Consent): Newsletter subscription, contact form consent, public display of user reviews on the website (username is shown in full; consent can be revoked at any time in account settings)
Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR (Contract performance): Registration, login, transaction management, budget management — to fulfill the usage agreement
Art. 6(1)(c) GDPR (Legal obligation): Retention of consent records pursuant to Art. 7 GDPR
Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR (Legitimate interests): Security logging (login attempts, account lockouts), abuse prevention, technical troubleshooting

sections.title: § 5 Legitimate Interests (Art. 13(1)(d) GDPR)
paragraphs: Where we base processing on legitimate interests (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR), we pursue the following interests:

Protecting our application from unauthorized access and misuse by logging login attempts and suspicious activities. Ensuring system stability and security through technical error logs. Your interests, fundamental rights and freedoms do not override these interests, as processing is limited to what is technically necessary and automatic deletion periods apply.

We have conducted and internally documented a Legitimate Interest Assessment (LIA). The result confirms that processing is limited to the technically necessary minimum (e.g., IP masking, deletion after 365 days) and does not disproportionately affect the rights of data subjects.

sections.title: § 6 Retention Periods and Deletion (Art. 13(2)(a) GDPR)
paragraphs: We store personal data only as long as necessary for the respective purpose:
list: Account data (username, email, password hash): Until account deletion by the user

Transaction data: According to the configured retention period (lifecycle policy); automatically deleted after expiry with prior email notification. Where transaction data is subject to statutory retention obligations, it will be stored in accordance with applicable legal periods (§ 147 AO: up to 10 years).
Security audit logs: 365 days, then automatically deleted via TTL index
Consent records (ConsentLog): 3 years (legal proof obligation under Art. 7(1) GDPR)
Unconfirmed newsletter subscriptions: 48 hours, then automatically deleted
Contact inquiries: Deleted after processing

sections.title: § 7 Cookies and Local Storage
paragraphs: We only use technically necessary cookies and local storage entries. No tracking cookies or third-party cookies are used.
list: accessToken (In-Memory): JWT access token for authentication — validity 1 hour; stored exclusively as a JavaScript variable in memory, not persisted (XSS protection)
refreshToken (httpOnly Secure cookie): Refresh token for token renewal — validity 7 days; httpOnly, Secure, SameSite=strict
privacyNoticeSeen (localStorage): Privacy notice acknowledgment status (read/not read)
theme (localStorage): Selected color scheme (light/dark)
i18nextLng (localStorage): Selected language
sidebar-collapsed (localStorage): Sidebar state

sections.title: § 8 Data Sharing with Third Parties
paragraphs: Your data is generally not shared with third parties. Sharing occurs only in the following cases:
When you have expressly consented (Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR), when sharing is necessary for contract performance (Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR), or when there is a legal obligation (Art. 6(1)(c) GDPR).
We use the following service providers as data processors (Art. 28 GDPR) to operate the application:
list: Hosting (Frontend & Backend/API): Render Services, Inc., 525 Brannan Street, Suite 300, San Francisco, CA 94107, USA. Data processing on servers in the EU/EEA region (Frankfurt). Standard contractual clauses pursuant to Art. 46(2)(c) GDPR.
Database: MongoDB, Inc. (MongoDB Atlas), New York, USA. Cluster location: EU (Frankfurt). Standard contractual clauses pursuant to Art. 46(2)(c) GDPR.
Newsletter delivery: Handled via our own SMTP server. No external newsletter service provider is used.
additionalParagraphs: We use data processors to operate our services. Written agreements pursuant to Art. 28 GDPR (Data Processing Agreements) are in place with all named service providers. Where personal data is transferred to third countries (e.g., USA), we use the Standard Contractual Clauses (SCC) approved by the European Commission and conduct Transfer Impact Assessments as well as, where necessary, supplementary technical and organizational measures to ensure a level of data protection comparable to the EU standard (cf. EDPB Recommendations 01/2020).

sections.title: § 9 Your Rights as a Data Subject
paragraphs: Under the GDPR, you have the following rights:
list: Right of access (Art. 15 GDPR): You can request information about your stored data
Right to rectification (Art. 16 GDPR): You can request correction of inaccurate data
Right to erasure (Art. 17 GDPR): You can request deletion of your data (account deletion in settings)
Right to restriction (Art. 18 GDPR): You can request restriction of processing
Right to data portability (Art. 20 GDPR): You can export your data in a machine-readable format (CSV export in the application)
Right to object (Art. 21 GDPR): You can object to processing based on legitimate interests
additionalParagraphs: To exercise your rights, please send an email to finora@yellowdeveloper.de or use the contact form at /contact. For identity verification, please include your username and, if applicable, additional information suitable for verification. We will respond to your request within one month (Art. 12(3) GDPR).

sections.title: § 10 Right to Withdraw Consent (Art. 13(2)(c) GDPR)
paragraphs: If the processing of your data is based on consent (Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR), you have the right to withdraw this consent at any time. The lawfulness of the processing carried out on the basis of consent until its withdrawal is not affected.
You can unsubscribe from the newsletter at any time via the unsubscribe link in every email or via the newsletter toggle in account settings.

The newsletter subscription uses a double opt-in process: after entering your email address, you will receive a confirmation email. Your subscription is only activated after clicking the link contained therein. Proof of consent (ConsentLog) is retained for 3 years (Art. 7(1) GDPR).

sections.title: § 11 Right to Lodge a Complaint (Art. 13(2)(d) GDPR)
paragraphs: Without prejudice to any other administrative or judicial remedy, you have the right to lodge a complaint with a data protection supervisory authority if you believe that the processing of your data violates the GDPR.

The supervisory authority responsible for us is: The State Commissioner for Data Protection of Lower Saxony (Die Landesbeauftragte für den Datenschutz Niedersachsen), Prinzenstraße 5, 30159 Hannover, Germany, https://www.lfd.niedersachsen.de
An overview of all data protection commissioners and their contact details can be found at: https://www.bfdi.bund.de

sections.title: § 12 Automated Decision-Making (Art. 13(2)(f) GDPR)
paragraphs: No automated decision-making, including profiling, pursuant to Art. 22(1) and (4) GDPR takes place. We do not use processes that are based exclusively on automated processing and have legal effect on you or similarly significantly affect you.
Budget notifications and transaction lifecycle management are based on simple threshold checks and do not constitute automated decision-making within the meaning of the GDPR.

sections.title: § 13 Data Security
paragraphs: We implement comprehensive technical and organizational measures to protect your data:
list: HTTPS encryption for all data transmissions
Password hashing with bcrypt (12 rounds of salting)
JWT-based authentication with short-lived access tokens (1h, stored exclusively in memory) and httpOnly Secure refresh cookies (7d, SameSite=strict)
Rate limiting and account lockout after failed login attempts
Content Security Policy (CSP), HSTS and other HTTP security headers via Helmet
MongoDB query injection prevention through input sanitization
Regular security audits (npm audit, dependency scans) and annual review of security measures
No storage of authentication data in localStorage or sessionStorage — access tokens are held exclusively as in-memory variables to minimize XSS risks

sections.title: § 14 Contact for Privacy Concerns
paragraphs: For questions about data protection, the processing of your personal data, or to exercise your rights as a data subject, you can contact us at any time by email at finora@yellowdeveloper.de or via our contact form at /contact. We will respond to your inquiry without delay, but at the latest within one month (Art. 12(3) GDPR).

sections.title: § 15 Data Breaches and Notification Obligations (Art. 33, 34 GDPR)
paragraphs: In the event of a personal data breach, we will notify the competent supervisory authority without undue delay, and no later than 72 hours after becoming aware of it, insofar as the breach is likely to result in a risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons (Art. 33(1) GDPR).
Where a breach is likely to result in a high risk to your personal rights and freedoms, we will inform you as the affected data subject without undue delay (Art. 34 GDPR).
Our internal procedures, responsibilities, and contact points for handling data breaches are documented internally.

features.title: Features
features.subtitle: Everything you need for smart financial management

items.title: Expense Tracking
items.description: Track every transaction, categorize your income and expenses across 22 categories, and gain deep insights into where your money goes.

items.title: Budget Management
items.description: Set a monthly budget limit and configure a threshold at which you'll be notified by email — helping you stay on top of your spending.

items.title: Dashboard & Reports
items.description: Interactive charts for income vs. expenses, monthly trends, and category breakdowns — plus summary cards for a quick overview.

items.title: Data Export
items.description: Export all your transactions as PDF or CSV from the Settings page — ideal for personal records, tax preparation, or accounting.

items.title: Search & Filter
items.description: Search your transactions by keyword, filter by category, type, or date range, and sort by date or amount.

items.title: Multi-Language Support
items.description: Use Finora in your preferred language — English, German, Arabic (with full RTL support), and Georgian. Switch anytime.

items.title: Guest Mode & Full Access
items.description: Start instantly without an account in Guest Mode, or sign up for free to get cloud storage, budget alerts, export, and multi-device access.

items.title: Light, Dark & System Theme
items.description: Choose between light, dark, or system theme — Finora automatically adapts to your system preferences.

items.title: Secure & Private
items.description: Your financial data is encrypted and secure. No data sharing with third parties, GDPR-compliant, and security alerts for account changes.

pricing.title: Pricing
pricing.subtitle: Finora is completely free. Choose how you want to use it.

plans.name: Full Access
extraFeatures: Automatic weekly & monthly reports via email
Security alerts on login & password changes

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.description: Finora was created to make personal financial management simple, accessible, and free for everyone. Many people lose track of their finances because existing tools are too complex or too expensive. Finora solves this problem with an intuitive interface that works without registration — and with a free account unlocks the full range of features.

whatIs.title: What is Finora?
whatIs.description: Finora is a free web app for personal financial management. You can track income and expenses, categorize them across 22 categories, and analyze them through an interactive dashboard with charts. Set a monthly budget limit and receive alerts when you exceed it. Export your data as PDF or CSV. Finora supports four languages (German, English, Arabic, Georgian) and offers a guest mode for instant use without an account.

audience.title: Who is Finora for?
audience.description: Finora is designed for anyone who wants to manage their finances simply and clearly — whether students, young professionals, families, or anyone seeking a better overview of their income and expenses. Thanks to guest mode, you can try Finora instantly without creating an account. For long-term use with cloud storage, budget alerts, and data export, free registration is available.

values.title: Our Values
items: Privacy: Your financial data is encrypted and never shared with third parties.
Transparency: Finora is completely free — no hidden fees, no premium plans.
Accessibility: The app supports four languages including RTL layout for Arabic and is optimized for screen readers.
Ease of Use: Clean design, intuitive navigation, and instant start without registration.

developer.title: Developer
developer.name: Yellow Developer
developer.description: Finora is developed and maintained by Yellow Developer — a passionate full-stack developer dedicated to building useful, beautiful, and accessible web applications.

tech.title: What Makes Finora Special
tech.description: Finora is built with modern technologies to give you the best possible experience. The app loads lightning-fast, works on any device — whether smartphone, tablet, or desktop — and can be installed directly on your home screen. Choose between light, dark, or automatic design. Finora is continuously developed and improved so you can focus entirely on your finances.

blog.title: Blog
blog.subtitle: Tips, insights, and updates about personal finance
blog.comingSoonDescription: We're working on great content about personal finance, tips for saving money, and updates about Finora. Stay tuned!

posts.title: Budgeting Made Easy: 5 Steps to Financial Clarity
posts.excerpt: A good budget is the foundation of financial freedom. Learn the key steps to effectively manage your income and expenses.

sections.title: Why a Budget Matters
content: Without a plan, most people spend more than they earn — not because they earn too little, but because they have no overview. A budget changes that. It's not a cage, it's a compass.
Studies show: people with a written budget save on average 20% more per month than those without. The reason is simple — awareness leads to better decisions.

sections.title: Step 1: Track Your Income
content: The first step is the easiest: list all sources of income. Salary, freelance earnings, rental income, government benefits — everything counts. Enter only your net income, meaning the amount that actually arrives in your account.
If your income fluctuates, use a realistic average of the last three months.

sections.title: Step 2: Categorize Your Expenses
content: Divide your expenses into two groups: fixed costs (rent, insurance, subscriptions) and variable costs (groceries, leisure, clothing). Fixed costs are hard to change in the short term — variable costs are your lever.
Use Finora to automatically categorize transactions. This way you see at a glance where your money is really going.

sections.title: Step 3: Apply the 50/30/20 Rule
content: One of the most proven budgeting methods is the 50/30/20 rule: 50% for essentials (housing, food, transport), 30% for personal wants (restaurants, hobbies, entertainment) and 20% for saving and debt repayment.
This split is a good starting point — adapt it to your reality. If you live in a big city, you might spend 60% on essentials. That's not failure, it's a signal to adjust.

sections.title: Step 4: Set a Savings Goal
content: A budget without a savings goal is just a spending list. Define concrete goals: emergency fund (3–6 months' salary), vacation, new tech, or long-term retirement savings.
'I'm saving for a trip to Japan in September' is more motivating than 'I'm somehow saving'. Concrete goals make the difference.

sections.title: Step 5: Review Regularly
content: A budget is not a document you create once and never look at again. Review it monthly. Has anything changed? New expenses? Pay raise?
Finora shows you with interactive charts how your spending develops over months. This way you spot trends before they become a problem.

sections.title: Conclusion
content: Budgeting is not a restriction — it is financial self-determination. With the right tools and a little discipline, you regain control over your money.
Start today: enter your first transaction in Finora and feel what having a real overview is like.

posts.title: Saving Strategies That Actually Work
posts.excerpt: Discover proven methods for saving — from the 50/30/20 rule to automated savings goals with Finora.

sections.title: Why Do So Many People Fail at Saving?
content: Most people want to save — but at the end of the month, nothing is left. This is rarely about income, usually about order: spend first, then save. Whatever's left rarely ends up in savings.
The solution: saving has to be the first expense, not the last.

sections.title: Pay Yourself First
content: The 'Pay Yourself First' principle is simple: as soon as your salary arrives, immediately transfer a fixed amount to a separate savings account. Only then do you plan your remaining expenses.
This method works because it automates saving and removes it from the realm of willpower. You don't save because you were strong enough — you save because the system does it.

sections.title: The 50/30/20 Rule for Savers
content: As a reminder: 20% of your net income should go to savings and debt repayment. At $2,000 net that's $400 per month — $4,800 per year.
Start smaller if needed. 5% is better than 0%. The goal is to establish a habit, not to be perfect immediately.

sections.title: Automated Saving with Finora
content: In Finora's settings you can set a monthly budget limit and a warning threshold. When you've reached 80% of your budget, Finora warns you — in time to be more conscious for the rest of the month.
Combine this with an automatic standing order to your savings account — and you have a completely automated savings system.

sections.title: Small Habits — Big Impact
content: Big saving success often doesn't come from a single decision, but from many small habits: buying coffee out less often, reviewing subscriptions, waiting 24 hours before impulse purchases.
The compound interest effect also applies to habits: small, consistent savings add up to impressive amounts over the years.

sections.title: Emergency Fund — Your Financial Foundation
content: Before investing or pursuing larger savings goals, build an emergency fund: 3 to 6 months' salary in an easily accessible account. This buffer protects you from unexpected expenses without having to take on debt.
Financial stress is one of the most common stressors in everyday life — with a buffer it loses its power over you.

sections.title: Conclusion
content: Saving is not a question of discipline — it is a question of systems. Automate what you can, track your progress with Finora and start small.
Every euro you save today works for you tomorrow. Start now.

posts.title: Finora Q2 2026: New Features and Improvements
posts.excerpt: A look at upcoming features — including expanded categories, improved export options and more.

sections.title: Getting Started
paragraphs: Welcome to Finora! You can get started right away — either in Guest Mode without registration or with a free account for full functionality.

In Guest Mode, your data is stored in the current browser tab (sessionStorage). Once you close the tab, the data is lost. With an account, your data is securely stored in the cloud and available across all your devices.
To create your first transaction, click 'Add Transaction', choose a type (income or expense), enter the amount, select a category, and save.

sections.title: Understanding the Dashboard
paragraphs: The dashboard gives you a quick overview of your finances. At the top, you'll see three summary cards: total income, total expenses, and your current balance.

Below that, you'll find interactive charts: a pie chart showing the ratio of income to expenses, a monthly trend view, and a category analysis for both income and expenses. Your five most recent transactions are also displayed for a quick overview.

sections.title: Managing Transactions
paragraphs: You can add, edit, and delete transactions at any time. Each transaction has a type (income/expense), an amount, a date, a category, and an optional description.
22 categories are available — depending on the transaction type, relevant categories are shown (e.g., Salary, Freelance for income; Groceries, Rent, Transport for expenses).

sections.title: Search & Filter
paragraphs: Use the search bar to find transactions by description. With filters, you can narrow down by category, transaction type (income/expense), and date range.
Sorting allows you to order transactions by date or amount — ascending or descending.

sections.title: Budget Alerts
paragraphs: With an account, you can set a monthly budget limit in Settings and choose an alert threshold (e.g., 80%).
Finora will automatically warn you when your spending reaches the chosen threshold or exceeds your limit. This helps you stay on top of your expenses and meet your financial goals.

sections.title: Settings
paragraphs: In Settings, you can customize your language (German, English, Arabic, Georgian), currency, date format, and theme (Light, Dark, or System).
With an account, additional options are available: email notifications, newsletter management, data retention, and profile management.

sections.title: Exporting Data
paragraphs: As a registered user, you can export all your financial data as PDF or CSV. Go to Settings and select your preferred export type.
The export includes all your saved transactions — ideal as a backup, for bookkeeping, or personal financial reviews.

sections.title: Guest Mode vs. Account
paragraphs: In Guest Mode, you can use all basic features: transactions, dashboard, search & filter, categories, language selection, and themes. However, your data is only stored in the current browser tab.
With a free account, you additionally get: cloud storage, multi-device access, budget alerts, email notifications, data export (PDF/CSV), data retention, and profile management.
Registration is free and takes only a few seconds.

sections.title: Need More Help?
paragraphs: If you can't find the answer you're looking for, feel free to contact us via email at <email>finora@yellowdeveloper.de</email> or use our <contactLink>contact form</contactLink>.
Also check out our <faqLink>FAQ</faqLink> — you'll find answers to the most common questions there.

questions.question: Is Finora really free?
questions.answer: Yes! Finora is 100% free — both Guest Mode and Full Access with a registered account. There are no hidden fees, no premium tiers, and no ads.

questions.question: Do I need to create an account?
questions.answer: No. You can use Finora right away in Guest Mode without registration. However, creating a free account gives you cloud storage, budget alerts, data export (PDF/CSV), email reports, and access from multiple devices.

questions.question: What is the difference between Guest Mode and Full Access?
questions.answer: In Guest Mode, you can track transactions, use the dashboard, search, filter and sort, and switch languages and themes. However, your data is only stored in the current browser tab (sessionStorage) and is lost when you close it. With a free account, you additionally get cloud storage, budget alerts, data export, email reports, and multi-device access.

questions.question: Is my financial data secure?
questions.answer: Yes. All data is encrypted in transit and stored securely. We use industry-standard security practices, never share your data with third parties, and send you security alerts for login and password changes.

questions.question: Which languages are supported?
questions.answer: Finora currently supports English, German, Arabic (with full RTL support), and Georgian. You can switch the language at any time in the settings or via the language switcher.

questions.question: Can I export my data?
questions.answer: Yes. With a registered account, you can export all your transactions as PDF or CSV from the Settings page. The export includes all stored transactions.

questions.question: What happens to my data in Guest Mode?
questions.answer: In Guest Mode, your data is stored in the browser's sessionStorage — meaning only in the current tab. Once you close the tab or browser window, all data is permanently lost. For persistent storage, we recommend creating a free account.

questions.question: How do I set up budget alerts?
questions.answer: Sign in with your free account and go to Settings. There you can set a monthly budget limit and configure a threshold at which you will be notified by email. Budget alerts are only available with a registered account.

questions.question: Can I use Finora on multiple devices?
questions.answer: Yes — with a free account, your data is securely stored in the cloud and accessible from any device. Simply sign in with your account and your transactions, settings, and budgets are instantly available.

questions.question: How do I contact support?
questions.answer: You can reach us by email at finora@yellowdeveloper.de or through the contact form on our Contact page. We aim to respond to all inquiries promptly.

sections.title: Dispute Resolution
paragraphs: The European Commission provides a platform for online dispute resolution (ODR): https://ec.europa.eu/consumers/odr/
We are neither willing nor obliged to participate in dispute resolution proceedings before a consumer arbitration board.

sections.title: Liability for Content
paragraphs: As a service provider, we are responsible for our own content on these pages in accordance with general laws pursuant to § 7 Abs.1 DDG. According to §§ 8 to 10 DDG, we are not obligated to monitor transmitted or stored third-party information or to investigate circumstances that indicate illegal activity.

sections.title: Liability for Links
paragraphs: Our website contains links to external third-party websites over whose content we have no influence. We therefore cannot assume any liability for such external content. The respective provider or operator of the pages is always responsible for the content of the linked pages.

privacyNotice.title: We respect your privacy
privacyNotice.description: We use only technically necessary storage and processing technologies to provide the application. In guest mode, data is stored locally in your browser. For registered users, personal data is processed and stored on our servers. No tracking or marketing cookies are used.

privacyNoticePage.title: Privacy Notice
privacyNoticePage.intro: This notice summarizes how Finora collects, processes, and protects your personal data. For detailed information, please read our full Privacy Policy.

sections.title: What Data We Collect
paragraphs: We collect account data (name, email, password hash), transaction data you enter, and technical usage data such as IP address, browser type, and device type.

sections.title: Why We Process Data
paragraphs: Your data is used exclusively to provide and improve our service, for authentication and account security, analytics, and to fulfill legal obligations.

sections.title: How We Protect Data
paragraphs: All data is transmitted (TLS) and stored encrypted. Passwords are hashed with bcrypt. We use HTTP-only cookies and implement CSRF protection.

sections.title: Cookies
paragraphs: Finora only uses technically necessary cookies for session management. Analytical cookies are only set after your explicit consent.

sections.title: Your Rights
paragraphs: You have the right to access, rectification, erasure, restriction of processing, and data portability. Contact us using the details below.

sections.title: Contact
paragraphs: For privacy-related questions, reach us at: finora@yellowdeveloper.de
